 Introduction to Automated Machine Learning




                                             3
 What is Automated Machine Learning (AutoML)?
Automated ML refers to automating the end-to-end process of applying ML to
real-world problems
Advantages
•   Accessibility: Makes ML accessible by automating its application
•   Ease of Use: Allows no expert users to develop effective ML models
•   Efficiency: Increases ML workflow efficiency
•   Time saving: Reduces the time required to produce predictive models
•   Facilitates rapid prototyping and testing in a business context




                                                                          4
 Machine learning Vs Automated Machine Learning
• AutoML is highly automated
• Traditional ML requires user intervention at different stages of ML
  workflow
• Traditional Machine Learning
 • Expertise Required: High level of expertise in data science
 • Process: Manual preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection,
   hyperparameter tuning
 • Time-Consuming: Each step requires multiple iterations to optimise model
   performance
 • Customisation: Allows deep customisation and fine-tuning
 • Barrier to Entry: High, requiring skilled analysts and complex tools

                                                                          5
  Machine learning Vs Automated Machine Learning
• Automated Machine Learning (AutoML)
 • Expertise Required: Less, accessible to non-experts
 • Process: Automates data preprocessing, feature selection,
   hyperparameter tuning, model training and evaluation
 • Time-Consuming: Reduces time by automating repetitive tasks
 • Customisation: Allows customisation, but less than manual methods
 • Barrier to Entry: Lowered, making ML more accessible
    ➢ Streamline model development, faster and user-friendly
    ➢ Offers in-depth analysis and customisation at the cost of complexity and time



                                                                                 6
 Why Automated Machine Learning Matters?
Enhance Model Validation
  • Uses robust techniques like cross-validation
  • Ensures models generalise well to unseen data
     • Automates workflow, speeding up processes, improving reliability
Facilitate Model Deployment
  • Streamlines deployment by packaging models automatically
  • Manages scalability and monitors performance
Broaden Accessibility
  • Reduces the need for technical expertise
  • Allows non-experts to develop and deploy models
  • Efficient use of resources, enabling wider ML adoption
                                                                      7
 Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning
Customer segmentation/personalisation
    •    Analyse customer data to identify distinct groups
    •    Tailor marketing strategies to each segment
         ➢ Increases engagement, loyalty, and conversion rates
Sales Forecasting
•       Predict future sales trends using historical data
         ➢ Aids in inventory management, budget planning, decision-making
Fraud Detection
•       Train AutoML algorithms to detect fraud patterns/fraudulent transactions
         ➢ Enhances security measures and reduces financial losses

                                                                              8
 Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning
 Supply Chain Optimisation
 •       Forecast demand, manage inventory, Optimise delivery routes
          ➢ Cost savings, better customer satisfaction
 Predictive Maintenance
 •       Predict equipment failures with sensor data
     •    Reduce downtime, quality control, save costs
 Dynamic Pricing
 •       Adjust prices in real-time based on supply and demand dynamics
          ➢ Increase revenue and market competitiveness
 •       Streamline HR processes: analyse candidates resumes, predict turnover
                                                                                 9
 Business Applications of Automated Machine Learning

 Customer Support Automation
 •   Chatbots for instant responses and efficient issue resolution
 Health and Safety Monitoring
 •   Analyse health wearable data
 Market Dynamics
 •   Detect patterns about market trends, data-driven decisions
 Content Recommendation
 •   Enhance user experience with personalised suggestions

using SAS Viya




                        11
 What is SAS?
SAS offers over 150 products within 10 solutions
•   Advanced Analytics, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
•   Augmented Analytics & Business Intelligence
•   Cloud, Data Management
•   DataOps, Fraud & Security
•   Internet of Things (IoT)
•   Marketing Analytics
•   Operationalising Analytics
•   Risk Management

                                                                     12
 What is SAS (Statistical Analysis System)?
 SAS is a programming language
 • Used for statistical analysis
 • Reads data from spreadsheets and databases
 • Generate results in tables or graphs
 • Among the top programming languages for Data Scientists,
   alongside R and Python




                                                              13
 What is SAS Data Mining and Machine Learning?
A suite of analytics tools and software solutions offered by SAS
•   Extract valuable insights from data
•   Create predictive models,
•   Implement ML algorithms to solve complex problems
•   Enhance decision-making

                 Available in SAS Viya Web-based solution
                   No software to install, it runs in the Cloud!


                                                                   14
 SAS Data Mining and ML Pipeline
Pipelines are structured flows of analytics actions
 With various node types
 •   Data Source Nodes
     Import data from various sources into
     SAS environment
 •   Data Preparation Nodes
     Clean, transform, select key variables
 •   Model Training Nodes
 •   Model Assessment Nodes

 Data Partition (training, validation, and test)




                                The Partition Data proportions can be
                                edited after creating a project, but this
                                must be done before the Data node is
                                executed. After the Data node has been
                                run, you can view the partition proportions,
                                but cannot make any changes until data
                                source is reloaded



                                                                           34
  Data Partition (training, validation, and test)
• Training Set: Used to train the model
 • Learn patterns, relationships, and structure from this data
• Validation Set: Used to tune the model
 • Adjust the model configuration, tuning hyperparameters
• Testing Set: Used to evaluate the final model
 • Provide an unbiased evaluation of the model’s performance
 • Assess how well the model generalises to unseen data


                                                                 35
 Data Partition (training, validation, and test)
Common Proportions (training-validation-test)
• 70-15-15 Split: Balanced datasets for training, with sufficient data
  for validation and testing
• 80-10-10 Split: Used for large datasets with substantial validation
  and testing data
• 60-20-20 Split: Used when more data is required for validation
  and testing to avoid overfitting and ensure reliable metrics

                                       44
 Feature Engineering




                      45
 What is Feature Engineering?
Features Individual measurable properties or characteristics of a phenomenon
Why Feature Engineering?
Extract relevant information from raw data to make ML algorithms work
effectively
 • Feature Selection Identify the most relevant features to use
 • Feature Transformation Apply mathematical transformations to features to make
   them more suitable for modelling
 • Feature Generation Generate new features from existing data
 • Dimensionality Reduction Reduce features number while preserving variance in
   the data

                                                                                   46
       Why Feature Engineering?




Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/gilpress/2016/03/23/data-preparation-most-time-consuming-least-enjoyable-data-science-task-survey-says/
                                                                                                                                               47
 Feature Selection
Select relevant features for model using criteria
 • AIC (Akaike Information Criterion)
 • BIC (Bayesian Information Criterion)
 • AICc (Corrected Akaike Information Criterion)
  • These criteria involve comparing values before and after including a
    variable in the model
  • They help decide whether a variable should be selected




                                                                       48
 Feature Transformation
Transform Data to Meet Model Assumptions
 • Normal Distribution: Ensure residuals follow a Gaussian distribution
 • Homoscedasticity: Ensure equal variance across data




   Source: https://communities.sas.com/t5/SAS-Communities-Library/Changing-the-Scale-Transforming-Data/ta-p/658278

                                                                                          99
 Model Hyper-Parameters Tuning




                                10
                                0
 What is Model Tuning?
Adjusting ML algorithm's hyperparameters to improve its performance
Hyperparameters of a decision tree:
Splitting options
 •   Grow Criterion used to split nodes.
     Common criteria include Gini impurity and entropy for classification tasks, and variance reduction for regression tasks.
 •   Maximum number of branches
     In binary trees, this is typically set to 2. Higher values can control the tree's branching factor for more complex trees.
 •   Maximum depth
     A deeper tree can model more complex patterns but might lead to overfitting. A smaller depth might underfit the data.
 •   Minimum leaf size
     Specifies the minimum number of observations required to form a leaf. This parameter can help prevent overfitting.
 •   Number of bins
     A higher number of bins allows for a more precise search for the best split but increases computational complexity.




                                                                                                                                  101
 What is Model Tuning?
Adjusting ML algorithm's hyperparameters to improve its performance
Hyperparameters of a decision tree:
Pruning options (subtree method)
 •   Cost Complexity Pruning (also known as Weakest Link Pruning)
     Trees are grown to their full size and then pruned back by removing subtrees that do not significantly
     improve the fit on the training data.
 •   Reduced Error Pruning
     The tree is fully grown using the training set, and then nodes are pruned (or removed) if doing so reduces
     the error on the validation set.




                                                                                                                  102
 Model Autotuning
• What is Autotuning?
 • Automate the identification of the hyperparameter values
   that give you the most accurate model
• Available for six of the Supervised Learning nodes:
 •   Bayesian Network
 •   Decision Tree
 •   Forest
 •   Gradient Boosting
 •   Neural Network
 •   SVM




                                                              103
 Autotuning Search Method
•   Grid Search: Evaluates a model with all combinations of
    hyperparameter values specified in a grid, allowing you to find the
    best combination.
•   Random Search: Randomly selects combinations of
    hyperparameters to evaluate, offering a more stochastic approach
    compared to the exhaustive nature of grid search.




                                         Parameter 2
            Parameter 2




                           Parameter 1                   Parameter 1
                          Grid search                  Random search      104
 Autotuning Search Method
•    Bayesian Optimization: Uses the Bayesian technique for global
     optimization to model the performance function of
     hyperparameters and then chooses the next hyperparameters to
     evaluate by optimizing a surrogate model.




    Source: Cao, Michael & Chen, Zhen & Jiang, Yi & Han, Yimo. (2022). Automatic parameter selection for electron ptychography via Bayesian optimization. Scientific
    Reports. 12. 12284. 10.1038/s41598-022-16041-5.
                                                                                                                                                                       105
 Autotuning Search Method
   •        Genetic algorithm: Inspired by the process of natural selection and
            genetics, where the fittest individuals are selected for reproduction
            in order to produce offspring of the next generation.

 Initialization              Evaluation                 Selection              Genetic Operators

                                                                                     Crossover
   Population                  Fitness                Selection for
                                                                                 (Recombination)
    Creation                 Calculation              Reproduction
                                                                                    + Mutation

                                                                                  Selected individuals are
                                                                                  paired and combined to
                                                                                   produce offspring. The
                                                                               crossover operation involves
                                                                               swapping parts of the genetic
                          Each candidate solution   Candidates that perform            representation
 Generating an initial
                              is evaluated to       the best are selected to    (hyperparameters) between
population of candidate
                           determine how well it        take part of the       two parents to create one or
       solutions
                                  performs           reproduction process              more children.

                                                                                 With a small probability,
                                                                               some parts of the offspring's
                                                                                genetic representation are
                                                                                    randomly altered.

                                                       127
 Challenges and Limitations of AutoML

Class discussion
• Complexity vs Simplicity
    AutoML aims to simplify the model-building process by automating many steps
    → limits the flexibility and customization of models

• Data Quality and Preprocessing
    AutoML attempts to handle data quality issues, but it might not always perform as well as
    manual preprocessing

• Scalability and cost considerations
    Depending on the complexity of the models and the size of the data, scalability and
    performance of AutoML might become bottlenecks
    Running extensive AutoML processes can be computationally expensive, leading to higher
    operational costs.
